title: Trends in Systems Biology for the Analysis and Engineering of Clostridium acetobutylicum Metabolism.
author_string: Yoo, Minyeong, Nguyen, Ngoc-Phuong-Thao, Soucaille, Philippe
abstract: Clostridium acetobutylicum has received renewed interest worldwide as a promising producer of biofuels and bulk chemicals such as n-butanol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,3-butanediol, isopropanol, and butyrate. To develop commercial processes for the production of bulk chemicals via a metabolic engineering approach it is necessary to better characterize both the primary metabolism and metabolic regulation of C. acetobutylicum. Here, we review the history of the development of omics studies of C. acetobutylicum, summarize the recent application of quantitative/integrated omics approaches to the physiological analysis and metabolic engineering of this bacterium, and provide directions for future studies to address current challenges.
